channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government announces anti-paper leak bill, appoints new Higher Education Secretary as Sonam Wangchuk ends fast amid NEET protests
item.description: Several significant developments took place overnight amid the ongoing protests over the alleged NEET paper leak. Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that the Union Cabinet would discuss a proposed...

item.title: Claude Virgin's Mid-Century Fashion Photography: Capturing British Elegance
item.description: Claude Virgin (1928–2006) was an influential American-born British fashion photographer whose work defined postwar British fashion photography during the 1950s and ’60s. Born in Atlanta, Georgia, he r...

item.title: Misinformation Circulates About Obama's Comments on Noncitizen Voting in 2016
item.description: The article addresses false claims circulating on social media that former President Barack Obama encouraged noncitizens to vote in the 2016 U.S. elections. These claims are based on a shortened clip ...

item.title: Jigawa resolves 47-year border dispute by resettling displaced farmers
item.description: The article details how the Jigawa State government, in collaboration with the National Boundary Commission (NBC), resolved a long-standing 47-year border demarcation dispute between Nigerian farmers ...

item.title: Olivia Attwood celebrates Savano drinks brand launch with celebrity rooftop event
item.description: Olivia Attwood, 35, made a striking fashion statement at a rooftop celebration for her new alcoholic drinks brand Savano, donning a skintight gold unitard as she mingled with celebrity guests includin...

item.title: Kenyan Passports: Tracking Methods and Key Information
item.description: The article explains how to track Kenyan passports using official methods like SMS, the eCitizen portal, and Posta tracking. It emphasizes the importance of using government channels to avoid scams an...

item.title: Actor reflects on challenges of portraying controversial character in 'The Polygamist'
item.description: South African actor S’dumo Mtshali, who plays Jonasi Gomora in the drama series 'The Polygamist,' has shared insights about the intense experience of embodying a complex character. The role, which por...

item.title: Cyril Ramaphosa's Interdict Halts Parliament's Impeachment Process
item.description: The Western Cape High Court has granted President Cyril Ramaphosa an interim interdict, halting parliament's section 89 impeachment process pending the outcome of his review application challenging th...

item.title: Vice President Sara Duterte's Overseas Trip: Clarifying the Reasons Behind Her Departure
item.description: The article clarifies that Vice President Sara Duterte left the country for meetings with overseas Filipinos and personal engagements, not because of the death of political vlogger Mima Alicia. A misl...

item.title: White House says Trump administration will not intervene in Andrew and Tristan Tate's extradition to the U.K.
item.description: The White House has stated that U.S. President Donald Trump will not intervene in the extradition proceedings involving Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan Tate, who are wanted in the United Kingdom t...

item.title: Technical access issue prevents reading news article
item.description: The article encountered a technical barrier preventing access, likely due to automated bot detection systems. News Corp Australia employs software to manage crawler bot traffic, which may inadvertentl...

item.title: Pastors Leverage Social Media for Ministry and Branding
item.description: The article discusses how modern-day pastors in Nigeria and globally are using social media platforms like Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook to expand their influence beyond traditional church settings....

item.title: California AG Blocks Paramount Merger Amid Antitrust Concerns and Legal Scrutiny
item.description: Attorney General Rob Bonta of California has taken legal action to block the proposed merger between Paramount Global and Skydance Media, citing antitrust concerns and potential harm to competition. T...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk Ends Hunger Strike After Government Assurances
item.description: The article discusses the end of a hunger strike by environmental activist Sonam Wangchuk in Delhi, which he concluded after receiving written assurances from the government regarding reforms to the N...

item.title: UK Economy Surpasses Growth Rates of Previous Decades
item.description: The UK's economy is experiencing its fastest growth in a decade, driven by a surge in consumer spending and business investment. Recent data from the Office for National Statistics (ONS) indicates tha...

item.title: Security forces conduct successful operation in Mastung, neutralizing four terrorists
item.description: In a significant security operation in Balochistan's Mastung district, Pakistani security forces have killed four terrorists and recovered weapons during a coordinated effort. The operation, confirmed...

item.title: AIADMK Leader Criticizes Tamil Nadu Government Over Unannounced Holiday for Movie Release
item.description: Senior AIADMK leader R B Udayakumar accused the Tamil Nadu government of implementing an unannounced holiday across the state to celebrate the release of Chief Minister M.K. Stalin's movie 'Jana Nayag...

item.title: Iten Court Lifts Fencing Orders in Kaptagat Forest Case, Allows Community Involvement
item.description: The Environment and Land Court in Iten has taken a significant step by admitting Community Forest Associations (CFAs) as interested parties in the ongoing legal battle over Kaptagat Forest fencing. Th...

item.title: Nigeria's Largest Meth Lab Seized in Federal Court Visit
item.description: A Nigerian federal court conducted an inspection of a clandestine methamphetamine laboratory in Ogun State, which is linked to the country's largest drug seizure operation. The visit followed the arre...

item.title: Trump's Financial Practices and Legal Risks Under Scrutiny
item.description: The article details Donald Trump's alleged financial misconduct and legal risks, focusing on his proposed 'Trump API' service that would allow Wall Street firms to pay $100,000 monthly for faster acce...
